creator: paul-christian.volkmer@mni.thm.de
text:
Starting CouchDB with '/usr/local/etc/rc.d/couchdb start' or on every system restart, CouchDB blocks on start and did not return to shell.

Fix: 

I found a patch for 8.2-RELEASE. The following lines have to be changed at the end of '/usr/local/etc/rc.d/couchdb'

Comment or remove : '#start_precmd=${name}_prestart'
New: 'case "$1" in *start) couchdb_prestart;; esac'

This works for me, but I still got some errors. The given PID-file path is incorrect and will cause CouchDB to fail on start. This can be fixed by replacing:
  'pidfile="/var/run/${name}/${name}.pid"'
with
  'pidfile="/var/run/${name}.pid"'
How-To-Repeat: * Start CouchDB with: '/usr/local/etc/rc.d/couchdb start'
* Restart system with installed and enabled CouchDB
